summarization.a: The figure shows the radial distribution function (RDF) for molten Al<sub>48</sub>O<sub>72</sub>, molten Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub>, and expanded alpha-Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> at the distange range of 0 - 10 Å.
summarization.b: The figure displays the  radial distribution function (RDF) for amorphous Al<sub>48</sub>O<sub>72</sub>, ALD alumina film, and anodized alumina film at the distance range of 1.0 - 3.5 Å.

a.question: Which curve differs the most from the two others?
Which sample looks most crystalline compared to others? What supports this conclusion?
a.answer:
"Expanded α-Al₂O₃" looks the most crystalline. It shows strong, sharp peaks, which is the kind of structure you expect from an ordered solid. By contrast, the molten alumina curves have much lower peak heights and are closer to 
g(r)≈1 at larger distances, consistent with a more disordered, liquid-like structure.

b.question: Which sample doesn't have a depletion of neighbors at 1 Å?
Which sample has the highest probability of finding a neighbor at 1.75 Å?
